content:
A primeira issue que está sendo feita do Firebase já está realizada, no painel já está habilitado para 100% e deveriam aparecer os personais disponíveis na aplicação quando fizesse uma busca e tivesse visto essa integração com o CMS.
1. Buscar todas as features a serem executadas, https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/issues
2. Cada uma das features possui detalhamento e os critérios de aceite bem definidos, esses critérios de aceite devem ser todos preenchidos em todas as tasks a serem realizadas
3. Ao finalizar cada uma das tasks deve realizar o /commit para deixar tudo bem segmentado e caso de problema consiga voltar
4. Após a finalização de todas as tasks crie o PR 
5. Após a conclusão e o PR tenha sido mergeado, as issues devem ser fechadas.

[{"assignees":[],"body":"## Visão geral

Este meta-issue consolida as issues que implementam o fluxo completo de personal trainer a partir da Home, conforme o design descrito abaixo.

## Fluxo completo

```
HOME
  │
  ├── [sem personal] Card CTA
  │         ↓
  │   PersonalTrainerView (já existe)
  │   └── TrainerListView (busca personais disponíveis)
  │             ↓
  │       ConnectionRequestSheet (solicita conexão)
  │             ↓ (após aprovação)
  │       Card atualiza para estado "com personal"
  │
  └── [com personal] Card com foto + nome + status
            │
            ├── tap no card → TrainerDashboardView
            │         ├── [Hoje]      Treino do dia em PDF  ← tab padrão
            │         ├── [Chat]      Chat com o personal
            │         └── [Histórico] Treinos anteriores
            │
            └── botão Chat → TrainerDashboardView (abre na aba Chat)
```

## Issues do fluxo

| # | Issue | Depende de | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| #28 | Card na Home (2 estados: sem/com personal) | #29, #30 | 🔲 |
| #29 | HomeViewModel: carregar estado do personal trainer | — | 🔲 |
| #30 | Rotas: `.trainerDashboard` e `.trainerChat` | — | 🔲 |
| #31 | TrainerDashboard: aba "Hoje" com PDF em destaque | #30 | 🔲 |

## Dependências de outras issues (FitToday-cms)

Para o fluxo funcionar de ponta a ponta, as seguintes issues do CMS precisam estar resolvidas:

| CMS Issue | O que desbloqueia |
|---|---|
| cms#11 — trainerId salvo no registro | Aluno aparece para o personal após conexão |
| cms#12 — token Firebase nas chamadas | FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase consegue buscar treinos |
| cms#14 — upload de PDF de treinos | Personal consegue enviar treino; aluno vê na aba Hoje |

## Ordem de implementação sugerida

```
1. #29 HomeViewModel (sem dependências de UI)
2. #30 Rotas (sem dependências de UI)
3. #31 TrainerDashboard aba Hoje (depende de #30)
4. #28 Card na Home (depende de #29 e #30)
```

## O que JÁ está implementado (não precisa criar)

- ✅ `PersonalTrainerView` — tela de busca e conexão
- ✅ `TrainerListView` — lista com search de personais
- ✅ `TrainerSearchView` — busca por nome ou código
- ✅ `TrainerCard.swift` — componente de card (compact + expanded)
- ✅ `ConnectionRequestSheet` — solicitar conexão
- ✅ `TrainerChatView` — chat com o personal
- ✅ `TrainerHistoryView` — histórico de treinos
- ✅ `CMSWorkoutDetailView` — detalhe do treino com PDF
- ✅ `PDFViewerView` — visualizador de PDF
- ✅ `GetCurrentTrainerUseCase` — buscar personal atual
- ✅ `FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase` — buscar treinos do CMS
- ✅ Rotas `.personalTrainer` e `.trainerSearch` no AppRouter
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":32,"title":"meta: Fluxo completo de conexão personal trainer na Home (issues #28–#31)"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

O `TrainerDashboardView` já existe com tabs de Chat e Histórico. Para o fluxo completo de personal trainer, a **aba principal deve ser o treino do dia** — o PDF enviado pelo personal que o aluno precisa ver e executar.

Atualmente não existe uma aba dedicada ao "treino de hoje" com visualizador de PDF como destaque.

## Comportamento esperado

### Tab "Hoje" (tab padrão, primeiro)

```
╔══════════════════════════════════╗
║  [Hoje] [Chat] [Histórico]       ║
╠══════════════════════════════════╣
║                                  ║
║  🏋️ Treino de Hoje               ║
║  "Hipertrofia — Peito e Tríceps" ║
║  Enviado por Carlos Silva        ║
║  Hoje às 08:30                   ║
║                                  ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────┐  ║
║  │                            │  ║
║  │        [PDF Preview]       │  ║
║  │                            │  ║
║  └────────────────────────────┘  ║
║  [ Ver PDF completo ]            ║
║                                  ║
║  ─────── Feedback ──────────     ║
║  Como foi o treino de ontem?     ║
║  ★ ★ ★ ★ ☆  [ Enviar ]         ║
╚══════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Estados da aba "Hoje"

**1. Treino disponível**
- Thumbnail/preview do PDF (primeiras páginas)
- Título, descrição e data de envio
- Botão "Ver PDF completo" → abre `PDFViewerView`
- Seção de feedback do treino anterior (se existir)

**2. Sem treino para hoje**
```
╔══════════════════════════════════╗
║  Nenhum treino para hoje         ║
║  Seu personal ainda não enviou   ║
║  o treino de hoje.               ║
║                                  ║
║  [ Ver treinos anteriores ]      ║
╚══════════════════════════════════╝
```

**3. Loading**
- Skeleton placeholder enquanto busca no CMS

## Implementação

### Nova tab e enum

```swift
// Adicionar tab "Hoje" como primeira tab
enum TrainerDashboardTab: CaseIterable {
    case today    // ← nova tab principal
    case chat
    case history
    
    var title: String {
        switch self {
        case .today:   return "Hoje"
        case .chat:    return "Chat"
        case .history: return "Histórico"
        }
    }
    
    var icon: String {
        switch self {
        case .today:   return "doc.fill"
        case .chat:    return "message.fill"
        case .history: return "clock.fill"
        }
    }
}
```

### TodayWorkoutTabView

Criar `Presentation/Features/PersonalTrainer/TodayWorkoutTabView.swift`:

```swift
struct TodayWorkoutTabView: View {
    let workout: TrainerWorkout?
    let isLoading: Bool
    let onViewPDF: (String) -> Void       // workoutId para abrir PDFViewerView
    let onViewHistory: () -> Void
    let onFeedbackSent: (CMSFeedbackRequest) -> Void
}
```

### Integração no PersonalTrainerViewModel

```swift
// Já existe FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase
// Filtrar por isScheduledForToday():
var todayWorkout: TrainerWorkout? {
    assignedWorkouts.first { workout in
        guard let date = workout.scheduledDate else { return false }
        return Calendar.current.isDateInToday(date)
    }
}
```

### Navegação para PDF

Quando o usuário toca em "Ver PDF completo":
```swift
router.push(.cmsWorkoutDetail(workout.id), on: .home)
// CMSWorkoutDetailView já existe e usa PDFViewerView
```

## Critérios de Aceite

- [ ] `TrainerDashboardView` tem 3 tabs: Hoje (padrão), Chat, Histórico
- [ ] Aba "Hoje" mostra o treino do dia se disponível no CMS
- [ ] Treino do dia exibe: título, descrição, data de envio e preview/thumbnail do PDF
- [ ] Botão "Ver PDF completo" navega para `CMSWorkoutDetailView` (já existente)
- [ ] Estado vazio (sem treino hoje) exibe mensagem + atalho para histórico
- [ ] Estado de loading exibe skeleton placeholder
- [ ] Seção de feedback do treino anterior aparece na aba "Hoje"
- [ ] Rota `.trainerChat` (#30) abre dashboard já na aba Chat
- [ ] Notificações push (quando implementadas) podem abrir diretamente a aba Hoje
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":31,"title":"feat: [TrainerDashboard] Aba 'Hoje' com treino do dia em PDF como destaque principal"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

O `AppRouter` já tem as rotas `.personalTrainer` (tela completa de busca/conexão) e `.trainerSearch`. Para o card da Home (#28) funcionar completamente, são necessárias duas novas rotas de acesso direto:

- `.trainerDashboard` — leva direto para o `TrainerDashboardView` do personal conectado
- `.trainerChat` — leva direto para a aba de chat do dashboard

## Mudanças no AppRouter

### 1. Adicionar rotas ao enum `AppRoute`

```swift
// AppRouter.swift
enum AppRoute: Hashable {
    // ... rotas existentes ...
    case trainerDashboard                    // Dashboard do personal (tabs: Hoje, Chat, Histórico)
    case trainerChat                         // Acesso direto à aba de chat
}
```

### 2. Registrar handlers no TabRootView

```swift
// TabRootView.swift — dentro do .navigationDestination
case .trainerDashboard:
    let viewModel = PersonalTrainerViewModel(resolver: resolver)
    TrainerDashboardView(viewModel: viewModel)

case .trainerChat:
    let viewModel = PersonalTrainerViewModel(resolver: resolver)
    TrainerDashboardView(viewModel: viewModel, initialTab: .chat)
```

### 3. Suporte a `initialTab` no `TrainerDashboardView`

O `TrainerDashboardView` precisa aceitar um parâmetro de tab inicial para que o acesso via `.trainerChat` já abra na aba correta:

```swift
struct TrainerDashboardView: View {
    @Bindable var viewModel: PersonalTrainerViewModel
    var initialTab: TrainerDashboardTab = .today  // nova tab padrão

    @State private var selectedTab: TrainerDashboardTab

    init(viewModel: PersonalTrainerViewModel, initialTab: TrainerDashboardTab = .today) {
        self.viewModel = viewModel
        self._selectedTab = State(wrappedValue: initialTab)
    }
}

enum TrainerDashboardTab {
    case today   // Treino do dia em PDF
    case chat
    case history
}
```

### 4. Deep link (futuro)

Adicionar suporte a deep link para essas rotas:
```
fittoday://trainer/dashboard
fittoday://trainer/chat
```

Registrar no `DeepLink.init?(url:)` para envio via push notification.

## Critérios de Aceite

- [ ] `.trainerDashboard` adicionado ao enum `AppRoute`
- [ ] `.trainerChat` adicionado ao enum `AppRoute`
- [ ] Ambas as rotas registradas no `.navigationDestination` do `TabRootView`
- [ ] `TrainerDashboardView` aceita `initialTab` como parâmetro
- [ ] `.trainerChat` abre o `TrainerDashboardView` já na aba de chat
- [ ] Deep link `fittoday://trainer/dashboard` e `fittoday://trainer/chat` funcionam (para push notifications futuras)
- [ ] Nenhuma regressão nas rotas existentes (`.personalTrainer`, `.trainerSearch`, etc.)
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":30,"title":"feat: [Navegação] Adicionar rotas de acesso direto ao Dashboard e Chat do personal a partir da Home"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

O `HomeViewModel` carrega perfil, entitlement, histórico e programas — mas **não carrega nenhum dado de personal trainer**. Para exibir o `PersonalTrainerHomeCard` (#28), o ViewModel precisa saber:

1. O usuário tem um personal conectado? Quem é?
2. Existe treino disponível hoje no CMS para esse aluno?

## Use Cases já disponíveis

Todos já existem em `Domain/UseCases/PersonalTrainerUseCases.swift`:

- `GetCurrentTrainerUseCase` → retorna `(trainer, relationship)?` se conectado
- `FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase` → retorna `[TrainerWorkout]` do CMS

## Mudanças no HomeViewModel

### 1. Novas propriedades observáveis

```swift
@Observable final class HomeViewModel {
    // ... propriedades existentes ...

    // Personal Trainer
    private(set) var personalTrainerCardState: PersonalTrainerHomeCardState = .loading
    private(set) var isPersonalTrainerEnabled = false
    private(set) var todayWorkoutId: String? = nil
}
```

### 2. Carregamento do estado de personal trainer

Adicionar no fluxo de `loadUserData()` — **em paralelo** com o carregamento de programas:

```swift
private func loadPersonalTrainerState() async {
    guard isPersonalTrainerEnabled else {
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        return
    }

    guard let getCurrentTrainerUseCase = resolver.resolve(GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol.self) else {
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        return
    }

    do {
        guard let (trainer, _) = try await getCurrentTrainerUseCase.execute() else {
            personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
            return
        }

        // Buscar treino do dia se cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled
        var todayWorkout: CMSWorkout? = nil
        if let fetchWorkoutsUseCase = resolver.resolve(FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCaseProtocol.self) {
            let workouts = try await fetchWorkoutsUseCase.execute()
            todayWorkout = workouts.first { isScheduledForToday(/bin/bash) }
            todayWorkoutId = todayWorkout?.id
        }

        personalTrainerCardState = .hasTrainer(trainer: trainer, todayWorkout: todayWorkout)

    } catch {
        // Falha silenciosa — não impede o restante da home de carregar
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        #if DEBUG
        print("[Home] Personal trainer state load failed: \(error)")
        #endif
    }
}

private func isScheduledForToday(_ workout: TrainerWorkout) -> Bool {
    guard let scheduledDate = workout.scheduledDate else { return false }
    return Calendar.current.isDateInToday(scheduledDate)
}
```

### 3. Integração no `loadUserData()`

```swift
// Carregar em paralelo com programas e histórico
async let personalTrainerTask: Void = loadPersonalTrainerState()
async let programsTask: Void = loadProgramsAndWorkouts(profile: profile)

_ = await (personalTrainerTask, programsTask)
```

### 4. Feature flag check

```swift
// Dentro de loadUserData(), junto aos outros feature flags
isPersonalTrainerEnabled = await featureFlags.isFeatureEnabled(.personalTrainerEnabled)
```

### 5. Observação em tempo real do relacionamento

Para que o card atualize automaticamente quando o aluno se conecta/desconecta do personal durante a sessão:

```swift
func startObservingTrainerRelationship() {
    guard let useCase = resolver.resolve(GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol.self) else { return }
    Task {
        for await relationship in useCase.observeRelationship() {
            await loadPersonalTrainerState()
        }
    }
}
```

## Critérios de Aceite

- [ ] `HomeViewModel` expõe `personalTrainerCardState: PersonalTrainerHomeCardState`
- [ ] `isPersonalTrainerEnabled` é verificado via `FeatureFlagChecking`
- [ ] `GetCurrentTrainerUseCase` é chamado durante `loadUserData()`
- [ ] Carregamento do personal trainer acontece **em paralelo** com programas (sem bloquear a Home)
- [ ] Falha no carregamento do personal trainer não impede o restante da Home de carregar
- [ ] Treino do dia é buscado via `FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase` quando personal está conectado
- [ ] `todayWorkoutId` é disponibilizado para navegação direta ao treino
- [ ] Relacionamento é observado em tempo real (card atualiza sem precisar fechar e abrir a Home)
- [ ] Todos os use cases são injetados via Swinject (sem instanciação direta)
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":29,"title":"feat: [HomeViewModel] Carregar estado do personal trainer e treino do dia do CMS"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

A `HomeView` hoje tem cards para treino com IA, streak e stats, mas **nenhuma menção ao personal trainer**. As telas de dashboard, chat, histórico e lista de trainers já existem em `Presentation/Features/PersonalTrainer/` — falta o card na Home que serve de entrada para esse fluxo.

## Comportamento esperado

O card de personal trainer deve aparecer na `HomeView` **somente quando a feature flag `personalTrainerEnabled` estiver ativa**, posicionado logo abaixo do `UserStatsSection`.

---

### Estado 1 — Usuário SEM personal conectado

```
╔════════════════════════════════╗
║  💪 Encontre seu Personal      ║
║  Treine com acompanhamento     ║
║  profissional e evolua mais    ║
║  rápido com treinos sob medida ║
║                                ║
║  [ Ver personais disponíveis ] ║
╚════════════════════════════════╝
```

- Título: "Encontre seu Personal Trainer"
- Subtítulo: copy motivacional (1-2 linhas)
- CTA: botão primário → navega para `PersonalTrainerView` (tela de busca/lista)
- Visual: ícone/ilustração relacionada a personal trainer

### Estado 2 — Usuário COM personal conectado

```
╔════════════════════════════════╗
║  [foto] Carlos Silva        ›  ║
║         Personal Trainer       ║
║         ● Online               ║
║                                ║
║  🏋️ Treino de hoje disponível  ║
║  [ Ver treino ]  [ Chat ]      ║
╚════════════════════════════════╝
```

- Foto + nome + especialização do personal
- Indicador de status (online/offline)
- Se houver treino do dia no CMS: badge "Treino disponível" + botão "Ver treino" → abre `CMSWorkoutDetailView`
- Botão "Chat" → navega direto para a aba de chat no `TrainerDashboardView`
- Tap no card → navega para `TrainerDashboardView` (visão completa com tabs)

---

## Implementação

### Componente: `PersonalTrainerHomeCard.swift`

Criar em `Presentation/Features/PersonalTrainer/Components/PersonalTrainerHomeCard.swift`:

```swift
struct PersonalTrainerHomeCard: View {
    let state: PersonalTrainerHomeCardState
    let onDiscoverTap: () -> Void
    let onDashboardTap: () -> Void
    let onChatTap: () -> Void
    let onWorkoutTap: () -> Void
}

enum PersonalTrainerHomeCardState {
    case noTrainer
    case hasTrainer(trainer: PersonalTrainer, todayWorkout: CMSWorkout?)
    case loading
}
```

### Integração na `HomeView`

No bloco `contentForState` (em cada case relevante — `workoutReady` e `workoutCompleted`), adicionar o card após o `UserStatsSection`:

```swift
// Apenas se personalTrainerEnabled == true
if viewModel.isPersonalTrainerEnabled {
    PersonalTrainerHomeCard(
        state: viewModel.personalTrainerCardState,
        onDiscoverTap: { router.push(.personalTrainer, on: .home) },
        onDashboardTap: { router.push(.trainerDashboard, on: .home) },
        onChatTap: { router.push(.trainerChat, on: .home) },
        onWorkoutTap: { router.push(.trainerWorkout(viewModel.todayWorkoutId), on: .home) }
    )
}
```

## Critérios de Aceite

- [ ] Card aparece na Home somente quando `personalTrainerEnabled = true`
- [ ] Estado "sem personal": card com CTA navegando para lista de personais
- [ ] Estado "com personal": card mostrando foto, nome, status do personal
- [ ] Estado "com personal" + treino disponível: badge + botão "Ver treino"
- [ ] Estado "com personal": botão "Chat" navega direto para aba chat
- [ ] Tap no card (com personal): navega para `TrainerDashboardView`
- [ ] Card respeita o design system: `FitTodayColor`, `FitTodayFont`, `FitTodaySpacing`
- [ ] Estado de loading (enquanto busca personal) mostrado discretamente
- [ ] Card não aparece se feature flag desligada (usuários sem acesso não veem nada)
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":28,"title":"feat: [Home] Card de personal trainer na Home — dois estados: sem personal e com personal"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

Toda a integração com personal trainer no app está implementada e protegida por **feature flags via Firebase Remote Config**. Enquanto as flags estiverem `false` (padrão), nenhuma tela de personal trainer aparece para os usuários.

## Flags que precisam ser habilitadas

| Flag | Descrição | Status atual |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| `personalTrainerEnabled` | Habilita toda a seção de personal trainer | `false` (default) |
| `trainerChatEnabled` | Habilita a aba de chat com o personal | `false` (default) |
| `cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled` | Habilita a busca de treinos via CMS REST API | `false` (default) |

## Como habilitar

1. Acessar Firebase Console → seu projeto → Remote Config
2. Adicionar os parâmetros acima com valor `true`
3. Publicar as mudanças

## Dependências antes de habilitar

Antes de ligar as flags em produção, garantir que:
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#11 resolvida (trainerId salvo no CMS)
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#12 resolvida (token Firebase enviado nas chamadas ao CMS)
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#13 resolvida (Stripe Webhook funcionando) **OU** fluxo de vinculação direta via Firebase implementado
- [ ] A URL do CMS está configurada corretamente (`CMSConfiguration.default.baseURL`)

## Sugestão: habilitar em staged rollout

- Fase 1: habilitar `personalTrainerEnabled = true` para % interno (testers)
- Fase 2: habilitar `cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = true` após validar CMS
- Fase 3: habilitar `trainerChatEnabled = true` após validar Firestore rules do chat

## Critérios de Aceite

- [x] Flags configuradas no Firebase Remote Config
- [ ] Seção "Personal Trainer" aparece no app após habilitar a flag
- [ ] Aluno consegue buscar e se conectar a um personal
- [ ] Após conexão, treinos atribuídos aparecem na tela do aluno
","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":27,"title":"feat: habilitar feature flags no Firebase Remote Config para ativar a integração com personal trainer"}]

O `HomeViewModel` carrega perfil, entitlement, histórico e programas — mas **não carrega nenhum dado de personal trainer**. Para exibir o `PersonalTrainerHomeCard` (#28), o ViewModel precisa saber:

## Use Cases já disponíveis

Todos já existem em `Domain/UseCases/PersonalTrainerUseCases.swift`:

- `FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase` → retorna `[TrainerWorkout]` do CMS

## Mudanças no HomeViewModel

### 1. Novas propriedades observáveis

```swift
@Observable final class HomeViewModel {
    // ... propriedades existentes ...

    // Personal Trainer
    private(set) var personalTrainerCardState: PersonalTrainerHomeCardState = .loading
    private(set) var isPersonalTrainerEnabled = false
    private(set) var todayWorkoutId: String? = nil
}
```

### 2. Carregamento do estado de personal trainer

Adicionar no fluxo de `loadUserData()` — **em paralelo** com o carregamento de programas:

```swift
private func loadPersonalTrainerState() async {
    guard isPersonalTrainerEnabled else {
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        return
    }

    guard let getCurrentTrainerUseCase = resolver.resolve(GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol.self) else {
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        return
    }

    do {
        guard let (trainer, _) = try await getCurrentTrainerUseCase.execute() else {
            personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
            return
        }

        // Buscar treino do dia se cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled
        var todayWorkout: CMSWorkout? = nil
        if let fetchWorkoutsUseCase = resolver.resolve(FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCaseProtocol.self) {
            let workouts = try await fetchWorkoutsUseCase.execute()
            todayWorkout = workouts.first { isScheduledForToday(/bin/bash) }
            todayWorkoutId = todayWorkout?.id
        }

        personalTrainerCardState = .hasTrainer(trainer: trainer, todayWorkout: todayWorkout)

    } catch {
        // Falha silenciosa — não impede o restante da home de carregar
        personalTrainerCardState = .noTrainer
        #if DEBUG
        print("[Home] Personal trainer state load failed: \(error)")
        #endif
    }
}

private func isScheduledForToday(_ workout: TrainerWorkout) -> Bool {
    guard let scheduledDate = workout.scheduledDate else { return false }
    return Calendar.current.isDateInToday(scheduledDate)
}
```

### 3. Integração no `loadUserData()`

```swift
// Carregar em paralelo com programas e histórico
async let personalTrainerTask: Void = loadPersonalTrainerState()
async let programsTask: Void = loadProgramsAndWorkouts(profile: profile)

_ = await (personalTrainerTask, programsTask)
```

### 4. Feature flag check

```swift
// Dentro de loadUserData(), junto aos outros feature flags
isPersonalTrainerEnabled = await featureFlags.isFeatureEnabled(.personalTrainerEnabled)
```

### 5. Observação em tempo real do relacionamento

Para que o card atualize automaticamente quando o aluno se conecta/desconecta do personal durante a sessão:

```swift
func startObservingTrainerRelationship() {
    guard let useCase = resolver.resolve(GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol.self) else { return }
    Task {
        for await relationship in useCase.observeRelationship() {
            await loadPersonalTrainerState()
        }
    }
}
```

","labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOQzKTKM8AAAACT9dr_w","name":"enhancement","description":"New feature or request","color":"a2eeef"}],"number":28,"title":"feat: [Home] Card de personal trainer na Home — dois estados: sem personal e com personal"},{"assignees":[],"body":"## Contexto

Toda a integração com personal trainer no app está implementada e protegida por **feature flags via Firebase Remote Config**. Enquanto as flags estiverem `false` (padrão), nenhuma tela de personal trainer aparece para os usuários.

## Flags que precisam ser habilitadas

| Flag | Descrição | Status atual |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| `personalTrainerEnabled` | Habilita toda a seção de personal trainer | `false` (default) |
| `trainerChatEnabled` | Habilita a aba de chat com o personal | `false` (default) |
| `cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled` | Habilita a busca de treinos via CMS REST API | `false` (default) |

## Como habilitar

1. Acessar Firebase Console → seu projeto → Remote Config
2. Adicionar os parâmetros acima com valor `true`
3. Publicar as mudanças

## Dependências antes de habilitar

Antes de ligar as flags em produção, garantir que:
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#11 resolvida (trainerId salvo no CMS)
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#12 resolvida (token Firebase enviado nas chamadas ao CMS)
- [x] Issue FitToday-cms#13 resolvida (Stripe Webhook funcionando) **OU** fluxo de vinculação direta via Firebase implementado
- [ ] A URL do CMS está configurada corretamente (`CMSConfiguration.default.baseURL`)

## Sugestão: habilitar em staged rollout

- Fase 1: habilitar `personalTrainerEnabled = true` para % interno (testers)
- Fase 2: habilitar `cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = true` após validar CMS
- Fase 3: habilitar `trainerChatEnabled = true` após validar Firestore rules do chat

## Critérios de Aceite

- [x] Flags configuradas no Firebase Remote Config
- [ ] Seção "Personal Trainer" aparece no app após habilitar a flag
- [ ] Aluno consegue buscar e se conectar a um personal
- [ ] Após conexão, treinos atribuídos aparecem na tela do aluno

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Here is the complete findings report:

---

## Complete Codebase Exploration Report — Personal Trainer Feature

### 1. PersonalTrainer Feature Files

**Location:** `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/PersonalTrainer/`

**Views:**
- `PersonalTrainerView.swift` — Top-level entry point. Conditionally renders `TrainerDashboardView` (when connected), `TrainerListView` (no trainer), or a pending/loading state. Handles connection request sheet and error alerts.
- `TrainerDashboardView.swift` — Dashboard with segmented tabs (Chat, History, Evolution). Shows trainer avatar header and a disconnect menu.
- `TrainerListView.swift` — Search bar + list of `TrainerRatingCard` components. Loads trainers on appear, has debounced search.
- `TrainerSearchView.swift` — Sheet-based trainer search.
- `TrainerHistoryView.swift` — Groups assigned workouts by "This Week / Last Week / Older". Renders `WorkoutHistoryCard` per workout.
- `TrainerEvolutionView.swift` — Metrics row (load change %, total workouts, frequency) + Swift Charts line/area chart.
- `TrainerChatView.swift` / `TrainerChatViewModel.swift` — Trainer chat interface.
- `CMSWorkoutDetailView.swift` / `CMSWorkoutFeedbackView.swift` — Detail and feedback for CMS-assigned workouts.

**ViewModel:**
- `PersonalTrainerViewModel.swift` — `@Observable @MainActor`. Holds `currentTrainer`, `connectionStatus`, `assignedWorkouts`, `searchResults`. Resolves all use case dependencies via `Swinject` `Resolver`. Checks `personalTrainerEnabled` and `trainerChatEnabled` feature flags on `onAppear`.

**Components:**
- `TrainerCard.swift` — Two variants: `.compact` (HStack with photo, name, chevron) and `.expanded` (with bio, specialization tags via `FlowLayout`).
- `TrainerRatingCard.swift` — Avatar + star rating + expandable bio + "View Profile" / "Rate" action buttons.

---

### 2. AppRouter and AppRoute Enum

**File:** `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Router/AppRouter.swift`

**`AppTab`** (5 tabs): `.home`, `.workout`, `.fitorb`, `.activity`, `.profile`

**Key `AppRoute` cases relevant to Personal Trainer:**
```swift
case personalTrainer    // → PersonalTrainerView
case trainerSearch      // → TrainerSearchView
case cmsWorkoutDetail(String)   // → CMSWorkoutDetailView
case cmsWorkoutFeedback(String) // → CMSWorkoutFeedbackView
```

**`AppRouter`** is `@Observable @MainActor`. Uses a `[AppTab: NavigationPath]` dictionary (`tabPaths`). Methods: `push(_:on:)`, `pop(on:)`, `select(tab:)`, `handle(deeplink:)`.

---

### 3. HomeViewModel

**File:** `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/HomeViewModel.swift`

Key state properties:
```swift
private(set) var journeyState: HomeJourneyState = .loading
private(set) var entitlement: ProEntitlement = .free
private(set) var topPrograms: [Program] = []
private(set) var historyEntries: [WorkoutHistoryEntry] = []
private(set) var todayWorkout: ProgramWorkout?
private(set) var todayProgramName: String?
private(set) var isAiWorkoutEnabled = true
```

There is **no `personalTrainer` state in HomeViewModel** — no `currentTrainer`, no personal trainer card. The feature flag `aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled` is checked, but `personalTrainerEnabled` is not checked here.

**Load flow:**
1. `onAppear()` → `loadUserData()` → checks feature flags → loads profile/entitlement → `loadProgramsAndWorkouts()` → `loadTodayWorkout()`
2. History, programs (top 4 recommended), and today's workout from the best program are loaded.

---

### 4. HomeView — Current Layout

**File:** `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/Home/HomeView.swift`

The layout (top to bottom):
```
ScrollView
  VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.lg)
    HomeHeader(greeting, date, isPro, goalBadgeText, userName, userPhotoURL, onNotificationTap)
    WeekStreakRow(completedDays, currentStreak)      [.padding(.horizontal)]
    DailyStatsCard(workoutsThisWeek, weeklyTarget, caloriesBurned, streakDays, totalSets, avgDuration)
    [if todayWorkout && .workoutReady]
      TodayWorkoutSection(workout, programName, onStartWorkout)
    contentForState  ← loading | noProfile | workoutCompleted | error views
```

**There is no Personal Trainer card in HomeView currently.** The personal trainer feature is only accessible via the navigation route `.personalTrainer` pushed from elsewhere (e.g., ProfileProView). There is a clear insertion point in the VStack — after `DailyStatsCard` or after `TodayWorkoutSection` — for a Personal Trainer card.

---

### 5. Use Cases for Personal Trainer

**File:** `/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Domain/UseCases/PersonalTrainerUseCases.swift`

| Protocol | Concrete Class | Purpose |

    10→
    11→enum AppTab: Hashable, CaseIterable {

    38→
    39→enum AppRoute: Hashable {
    40→    case onboarding
    41→    case editProfile  // Rota separada para edição de perfil existente
    42→    case setup
    43→    case workoutPlan(UUID)
    44→    case exerciseDetail
    45→    case workoutExecution  // Nova view de execução com timer e pause/play
    46→    case workoutExercisePreview(ExercisePrescription)  // Preview de exercício (não altera índice)
    47→    case workoutPreview(ProgramWorkout)  // Preview de treino antes de iniciar (Task 7.0)
    48→    case workoutSummary
    49→    case paywall
    50→    case programDetail(String)  // Detalhe do programa
    51→    case programWorkoutDetail(ProgramWorkout)  // Detalhe de um treino dentro do programa (Wger exercises)
    52→    case libraryWorkoutDetail(String)  // Detalhe de um treino da biblioteca (por ID)
    53→    case programExerciseDetail(ExercisePrescription)  // Detalhe de exercício na biblioteca/programa
    54→    case apiKeySettings  // Configuração de chave de API do usuário
    55→    case healthKitSettings // Integração Apple Health (PRO)
    56→    case privacySettings  // Privacy settings for social features
    57→    case authentication(inviteContext: String?)  // Authentication flow
    58→    case groupInvite(groupId: String)  // Group invitation deep link
    59→    case notifications  // Notifications screen for challenges
    60→    case customWorkouts  // Custom workout templates list
    61→    case customWorkoutBuilder(UUID?)  // Create or edit custom workout (nil = new)
    62→    case personalTrainer  // Personal Trainer connection and workouts
    63→    case trainerSearch  // Search for personal trainers
    64→    case cmsWorkoutDetail(String)  // CMS workout detail by ID
    65→    case cmsWorkoutFeedback(String)  // CMS workout feedback by ID
    66→    case aiChat  // AI Fitness Assistant (FitOrb)
    67→}
    68→
    69→struct DeepLink {
    70→    enum Destination {
    71→        case home
    72→        case onboarding
    73→        case setup
    74→        case paywall
    75→        case groupInvite(groupId: String)
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    let destination: Destination
    79→
    80→    init?(url: URL) {
    81→        guard let scheme = url.scheme?.lowercased(), scheme == "fittoday" else {
    82→            return nil
    83→        }
    84→
    85→        let host = url.host?.lowercased() ?? ""
    86→        let path = url.path.lowercased()
    87→
    88→        // Handle group invite: fittoday://group/invite/{groupId}
    89→        if host == "group", path.hasPrefix("/invite/") {
    90→            let groupId = String(path.dropFirst("/invite/".count))
    91→            destination = .groupInvite(groupId: groupId)
    92→            return
    93→        }
    94→
    95→        // Handle other routes
    96→        let pathOrHost = !host.isEmpty ? host : path
    97→        switch pathOrHost {
    98→        case "home", "/home":
    99→            destination = .home
   100→        case "onboarding", "/onboarding":
   101→            destination = .onboarding
   102→        case "setup", "/setup":
   103→            destination = .setup
   104→        case "paywall", "/paywall":
   105→            destination = .paywall
   106→        default:
   107→            return nil
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→}
   111→
   112→@MainActor
   113→protocol AppRouting: AnyObject {
   114→    var selectedTab: AppTab { get set }
   115→    var tabPaths: [AppTab: NavigationPath] { get set }
   116→
   117→    func select(tab: AppTab)
   118→    func push(_ route: AppRoute, on tab: AppTab?)
   119→    func pop(on tab: AppTab?)
   120→    func handle(deeplink: DeepLink)
   121→}
   122→
   123→// 💡 Learn: Navegação com @Observable permite melhor performance
   124→@MainActor
   125→@Observable final class AppRouter: AppRouting {
   126→    var selectedTab: AppTab = .home
   127→    var tabPaths: [AppTab: NavigationPath] = [:]
   128→
   129→    init() {
   130→        AppTab.allCases.forEach { tab in
   131→            tabPaths[tab] = NavigationPath()
   132→        }
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    func select(tab: AppTab) {
   136→        selectedTab = tab
   137→    }
   138→
   139→    func push(_ route: AppRoute, on tab: AppTab? = nil) {
   140→        let target = tab ?? selectedTab
   141→        if tabPaths[target] == nil {
   142→            tabPaths[target] = NavigationPath()
   143→        }
   144→        tabPaths[target]?.append(route)
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    func pop(on tab: AppTab? = nil) {
   148→        let target = tab ?? selectedTab
   149→        guard var path = tabPaths[target], !path.isEmpty else {
   150→            return
   151→        }
   152→        path.removeLast()
   153→        tabPaths[target] = path
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    func handle(deeplink: DeepLink) {
   157→        switch deeplink.destination {
   158→        case .home:
   159→            select(tab: .home)
   160→        case .onboarding:
   161→            select(tab: .home)
   162→            push(.onboarding, on: .home)
   163→        case .setup:
   164→            select(tab: .home)
   165→            push(.setup, on: .home)
   166→        case .paywall:
   167→            select(tab: .home)
   168→            push(.paywall, on: .home)
   169→        case .groupInvite(let groupId):
   170→            select(tab: .home)
   171→            push(.groupInvite(groupId: groupId), on: .home)
   172→        }
   173→    }
   174→
   175→    func handle(url: URL) {
   176→        guard let link = DeepLink(url: url) else { return }
   177→        handle(deeplink: link)
   178→    }
   179→}
   180→
   181→

1→//
     2→//  PersonalTrainerViewModel.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by AI on 04/02/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→import Swinject
    10→
    11→@Observable
    12→@MainActor
    13→final class PersonalTrainerViewModel {
    14→    // MARK: - State
    15→
    16→    private(set) var currentTrainer: PersonalTrainer?
    17→    private(set) var connectionStatus: TrainerConnectionStatus?
    18→    private(set) var relationshipId: String?
    19→    private(set) var assignedWorkouts: [TrainerWorkout] = []
    20→    private(set) var searchResults: [PersonalTrainer] = []
    21→    var searchQuery: String = ""
    22→    var inviteCode: String = ""
    23→    private(set) var isLoading: Bool = false
    24→    private(set) var isSearching: Bool = false
    25→    private(set) var isRequestingConnection: Bool = false
    26→    private(set) var error: Error?
    27→    var showConnectionSheet: Bool = false
    28→    var selectedTrainer: PersonalTrainer?
    29→    private(set) var isFeatureEnabled: Bool = false
    30→    private(set) var isChatEnabled: Bool = false
    31→
    32→    // MARK: - Dependencies
    33→
    34→    private let discoverTrainersUseCase: DiscoverTrainersUseCaseProtocol?
    35→    private let requestConnectionUseCase: RequestTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol?
    36→    private let cancelConnectionUseCase: CancelTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol?
    37→    private let getCurrentTrainerUseCase: GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol?
    38→    private let fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase: FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCaseProtocol?
    39→    private let fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase: FetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase?
    40→    private let featureFlagChecker: FeatureFlagChecking?
    41→    private let cmsWorkoutRepository: CMSWorkoutRepository?
    42→    private let authRepository: AuthenticationRepository?
    43→
    44→    private var observationTask: Task<Void, Never>?
    45→    private var workoutsObservationTask: Task<Void, Never>?
    46→
    47→    // MARK: - Initialization
    48→
    49→    init(resolver: Resolver) {
    50→        self.discoverTrainersUseCase = resolver.resolve(DiscoverTrainersUseCaseProtocol.self)
    51→        self.requestConnectionUseCase = resolver.resolve(RequestTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol.self)
    52→        self.cancelConnectionUseCase = resolver.resolve(CancelTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol.self)
    53→        self.getCurrentTrainerUseCase = resolver.resolve(GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol.self)
    54→        self.fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase = resolver.resolve(FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCaseProtocol.self)
    55→        self.fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase = resolver.resolve(FetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase.self)
    56→        self.featureFlagChecker = resolver.resolve(FeatureFlagChecking.self)
    57→        self.cmsWorkoutRepository = resolver.resolve(CMSWorkoutRepository.self)
    58→        self.authRepository = resolver.resolve(AuthenticationRepository.self)
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    // For testing
    62→    init(
    63→        discoverTrainersUseCase: DiscoverTrainersUseCaseProtocol? = nil,
    64→        requestConnectionUseCase: RequestTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol? = nil,
    65→        cancelConnectionUseCase: CancelTrainerConnectionUseCaseProtocol? = nil,
    66→        getCurrentTrainerUseCase: GetCurrentTrainerUseCaseProtocol? = nil,
    67→        fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase: FetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCaseProtocol? = nil,
    68→        fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase: FetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase? = nil,
    69→        featureFlagChecker: FeatureFlagChecking? = nil,
    70→        cmsWorkoutRepository: CMSWorkoutRepository? = nil,
    71→        authRepository: AuthenticationRepository? = nil
    72→    ) {
    73→        self.discoverTrainersUseCase = discoverTrainersUseCase
    74→        self.requestConnectionUseCase = requestConnectionUseCase
    75→        self.cancelConnectionUseCase = cancelConnectionUseCase
    76→        self.getCurrentTrainerUseCase = getCurrentTrainerUseCase
    77→        self.fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase = fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase
    78→        self.fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase = fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase
    79→        self.featureFlagChecker = featureFlagChecker
    80→        self.cmsWorkoutRepository = cmsWorkoutRepository
    81→        self.authRepository = authRepository
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    // Note: Task cancellation is handled by onDisappear()
    85→    // deinit removed to avoid @MainActor isolation issues
    86→
    87→    // MARK: - Computed Properties
    88→
    89→    var isConnected: Bool {
    90→        connectionStatus == .active
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    var isPending: Bool {
    94→        connectionStatus == .pending
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    var hasTrainer: Bool {
    98→        currentTrainer != nil
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    var canSearch: Bool {
   102→        !searchQuery.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    var canUseInviteCode: Bool {
   106→        !inviteCode.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    var pendingWorkoutsCount: Int {
   110→        assignedWorkouts.filter { $0.isActive }.count
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    // MARK: - Lifecycle
   114→
   115→    func onAppear() {
   116→        Task {
   117→            await checkFeatureFlag()
   118→            if isFeatureEnabled {
   119→                await loadCurrentTrainer()
   120→                startObservingRelationship()
   121→                startObservingWorkouts()
   122→            }
   123→        }
   124→    }
   125→
   126→    func onDisappear() {
   127→        observationTask?.cancel()
   128→        workoutsObservationTask?.cancel()
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    // MARK: - Feature Flag
   132→
   133→    private func checkFeatureFlag() async {
   134→        guard let checker = featureFlagChecker else {
   135→            isFeatureEnabled = false
   136→            return
   137→        }
   138→        isFeatureEnabled = await checker.isFeatureEnabled(.personalTrainerEnabled)
   139→        isChatEnabled = await checker.isFeatureEnabled(.trainerChatEnabled)
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    // MARK: - Load Current Trainer
   143→
   144→    func loadCurrentTrainer() async {
   145→        guard let useCase = getCurrentTrainerUseCase else {
   146→            #if DEBUG
   147→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] GetCurrentTrainerUseCase not available")
   148→            #endif
   149→            return
   150→        }
   151→
   152→        isLoading = true
   153→        error = nil
   154→
   155→        do {
   156→            if let result = try await useCase.execute() {
   157→                currentTrainer = result.trainer
   158→                connectionStatus = result.relationship.status
   159→                relationshipId = result.relationship.id
   160→                await loadAssignedWorkouts()
   161→            } else {
   162→                currentTrainer = nil
   163→                connectionStatus = nil
   164→                relationshipId = nil
   165→                assignedWorkouts = []
   166→            }
   167→        } catch {
   168→            self.error = error
   169→            #if DEBUG
   170→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error loading trainer: \(error)")
   171→            #endif
   172→        }
   173→
   174→        isLoading = false
   175→    }
   176→
   177→    // MARK: - Search Trainers
   178→
   179→    func searchTrainers() async {
   180→        guard let useCase = discoverTrainersUseCase else {
   181→            #if DEBUG
   182→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] DiscoverTrainersUseCase not available")
   183→            #endif
   184→            return
   185→        }
   186→
   187→        let query = searchQuery.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
   188→
   189→        isSearching = true
   190→        error = nil
   191→
   192→        do {
   193→            if query.isEmpty {
   194→                // Load all active trainers from CMS marketplace
   195→                searchResults = try await useCase.searchByName("", limit: 20)
   196→            } else {
   197→                searchResults = try await useCase.searchByName(query, limit: 20)
   198→            }
   199→        } catch {
   200→            self.error = error
   201→            searchResults = []
   202→            #if DEBUG
   203→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error searching trainers: \(error)")
   204→            #endif
   205→        }
   206→
   207→        isSearching = false
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    // MARK: - Find by Invite Code
   211→
   212→    func findByInviteCode() async {
   213→        guard let useCase = discoverTrainersUseCase else {
   214→            #if DEBUG
   215→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] DiscoverTrainersUseCase not available")
   216→            #endif
   217→            return
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        let code = inviteCode.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
   221→        guard !code.isEmpty else {
   222→            return
   223→        }
   224→
   225→        isSearching = true
   226→        error = nil
   227→
   228→        do {
   229→            if let trainer = try await useCase.findByInviteCode(code) {
   230→                selectedTrainer = trainer
   231→                showConnectionSheet = true
   232→            } else {
   233→                error = PersonalTrainerError.invalidInviteCode
   234→            }
   235→        } catch {
   236→            self.error = error
   237→            #if DEBUG
   238→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error finding by invite code: \(error)")
   239→            #endif
   240→        }
   241→
   242→        isSearching = false
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    // MARK: - Request Connection
   246→
   247→    func requestConnection(to trainer: PersonalTrainer) async -> Bool {
   248→        guard let useCase = requestConnectionUseCase else {
   249→            #if DEBUG
   250→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] RequestTrainerConnectionUseCase not available")
   251→            #endif
   252→            return false
   253→        }
   254→
   255→        isRequestingConnection = true
   256→        error = nil
   257→
   258→        do {
   259→            let newRelationshipId = try await useCase.execute(trainerId: trainer.id)
   260→            relationshipId = newRelationshipId
   261→            currentTrainer = trainer
   262→            connectionStatus = .pending
   263→            showConnectionSheet = false
   264→            selectedTrainer = nil
   265→            searchResults = []
   266→            searchQuery = ""
   267→            inviteCode = ""
   268→
   269→            // Register student in CMS so the trainer can assign workouts
   270→            await registerStudentInCMS(trainerId: trainer.id)
   271→
   272→            return true
   273→        } catch {
   274→            self.error = error
   275→            #if DEBUG
   276→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error requesting connection: \(error)")
   277→            #endif
   278→            return false
   279→        }
   280→    }
   281→
   282→    // MARK: - CMS Student Registration
   283→
   284→    /// Registers the student in the CMS after a successful Firebase connection.
   285→    /// This is best-effort — failure does not block the connection flow.
   286→    private func registerStudentInCMS(trainerId: String) async {
   287→        guard let repo = cmsWorkoutRepository,
   288→              let authRepo = authRepository else {
   289→            #if DEBUG
   290→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] CMS registration skipped: dependencies not available")
   291→            #endif
   292→            return
   293→        }
   294→
   295→        do {
   296→            guard let user = try await authRepo.currentUser() else {
   297→                #if DEBUG
   298→                print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] CMS registration skipped: no current user")
   299→                #endif
   300→                return
   301→            }
   302→
   303→            try await repo.registerStudent(
   304→                firebaseUid: user.id,
   305→                trainerId: trainerId,
   306→                displayName: user.displayName,
   307→                email: user.email
   308→            )
   309→
   310→            #if DEBUG
   311→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Student registered in CMS for trainer \(trainerId)")
   312→            #endif
   313→        } catch {
   314→            #if DEBUG
   315→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] CMS registration failed (non-blocking): \(error)")
   316→            #endif
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    // MARK: - Cancel Connection
   321→
   322→    func cancelConnection() async -> Bool {
   323→        guard let useCase = cancelConnectionUseCase,
   324→              let relId = relationshipId else {
   325→            #if DEBUG
   326→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] CancelTrainerConnectionUseCase not available or no relationship")
   327→            #endif
   328→            return false
   329→        }
   330→
   331→        isLoading = true
   332→        error = nil
   333→
   334→        do {
   335→            try await useCase.execute(relationshipId: relId)
   336→            currentTrainer = nil
   337→            connectionStatus = nil
   338→            relationshipId = nil
   339→            assignedWorkouts = []
   340→            return true
   341→        } catch {
   342→            self.error = error
   343→            #if DEBUG
   344→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error canceling connection: \(error)")
   345→            #endif
   346→            return false
   347→        }
   348→    }
   349→
   350→    // MARK: - Load Assigned Workouts
   351→
   352→    private func loadAssignedWorkouts() async {
   353→        // Try CMS workouts first (primary source)
   354→        if let cmsUseCase = fetchCMSWorkoutsUseCase {
   355→            do {
   356→                let result = try await cmsUseCase.execute()
   357→                assignedWorkouts = result.workouts
   358→                #if DEBUG
   359→                print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Loaded \(result.workouts.count) CMS workouts")
   360→                #endif
   361→                return
   362→            } catch {
   363→                #if DEBUG
   364→                print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] CMS workouts failed, falling back to Firebase: \(error)")
   365→                #endif
   366→            }
   367→        }
   368→
   369→        // Fallback to Firebase workouts
   370→        guard let useCase = fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase else {
   371→            return
   372→        }
   373→
   374→        do {
   375→            assignedWorkouts = try await useCase.execute()
   376→        } catch {
   377→            #if DEBUG
   378→            print("[PersonalTrainerViewModel] Error loading workouts: \(error)")
   379→            #endif
   380→        }
   381→    }
   382→
   383→    // MARK: - Observation
   384→
   385→    private func startObservingRelationship() {
   386→        observationTask?.cancel()
   387→        observationTask = Task {
   388→            guard let useCase = getCurrentTrainerUseCase else { return }
   389→
   390→            for await relationship in useCase.observeRelationship() {
   391→                guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
   392→
   393→                if let rel = relationship {
   394→                    connectionStatus = rel.status
   395→                    relationshipId = rel.id
   396→
   397→                    // Reload trainer if status changed
   398→                    if rel.status == .active && currentTrainer == nil {
   399→                        await loadCurrentTrainer()
   400→                    }
   401→                } else {
   402→                    currentTrainer = nil
   403→                    connectionStatus = nil
   404→                    relationshipId = nil
   405→                }
   406→            }
   407→        }
   408→    }
   409→
   410→    private func startObservingWorkouts() {
   411→        workoutsObservationTask?.cancel()
   412→        workoutsObservationTask = Task {
   413→            guard let useCase = fetchAssignedWorkoutsUseCase else { return }
   414→
   415→            for await workouts in useCase.observe() {
   416→                guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
   417→                assignedWorkouts = workouts
   418→            }
   419→        }
   420→    }
   421→
   422→    // MARK: - UI Helpers
   423→
   424→    func selectTrainer(_ trainer: PersonalTrainer) {
   425→        selectedTrainer = trainer
   426→        showConnectionSheet = true
   427→    }
   428→
   429→    func dismissConnectionSheet() {
   430→        showConnectionSheet = false
   431→        selectedTrainer = nil
   432→    }
   433→
   434→    func clearError() {
   435→        error = nil
   436→    }
   437→
   438→    func clearSearch() {
   439→        searchQuery = ""
   440→        Task { await searchTrainers() }
   441→    }
   442→}
   443→

1→//
     2→//  HomeView.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by AI on 05/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import SwiftUI
     9→import Swinject
    10→
    11→struct HomeView: View {
    12→    @Environment(AppRouter.self) private var router
    13→
    14→    @State private var viewModel: HomeViewModel
    15→
    16→    init(resolver: Resolver?) {
    17→        guard let resolver = resolver else {
    18→            fatalError("Resolver is required for HomeView")
    19→        }
    20→        self._viewModel = State(wrappedValue: HomeViewModel(resolver: resolver))
    21→    }
    22→
    23→    var body: some View {
    24→        ScrollView {
    25→            VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.lg) {
    26→                // Header with greeting
    27→                HomeHeader(
    28→                    greeting: viewModel.greeting,
    29→                    dateFormatted: viewModel.currentDateFormatted,
    30→                    isPro: viewModel.entitlement.isPro,
    31→                    goalBadgeText: viewModel.goalBadgeText,
    32→                    userName: viewModel.userName,
    33→                    userPhotoURL: viewModel.userPhotoURL,
    34→                    onNotificationTap: { router.push(.notifications, on: .home) }
    35→                )
    36→
    37→                // Week streak circles
    38→                WeekStreakRow(
    39→                    completedDays: viewModel.weekCompletedDays,
    40→                    currentStreak: viewModel.streakDays
    41→                )
    42→                .padding(.horizontal)
    43→
    44→                // Daily stats summary
    45→                DailyStatsCard(
    46→                    workoutsThisWeek: viewModel.workoutsThisWeek,
    47→                    weeklyTarget: viewModel.weeklyTarget,
    48→                    caloriesBurned: viewModel.caloriesBurnedFormatted,
    49→                    streakDays: viewModel.streakDays,
    50→                    totalSets: viewModel.totalSetsThisWeek,
    51→                    avgDuration: viewModel.avgDurationMinutes
    52→                )
    53→
    54→                // Today's workout section
    55→                if let todayWorkout = viewModel.todayWorkout,
    56→                   case .workoutReady = viewModel.journeyState {
    57→                    TodayWorkoutSection(
    58→                        workout: todayWorkout,
    59→                        programName: viewModel.todayProgramName,
    60→                        onStartWorkout: {
    61→                            router.push(.workoutPreview(todayWorkout), on: .home)
    62→                        }
    63→                    )
    64→                }
    65→
    66→                // Content based on journey state
    67→                contentForState
    68→            }
    69→            .padding(.bottom, FitTodaySpacing.xl)
    70→        }
    71→        .background(FitTodayColor.background.ignoresSafeArea())
    72→        .navigationBarHidden(true)
    73→        .task {
    74→            viewModel.onAppear()
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    // MARK: - Content for State
    79→
    80→    @ViewBuilder
    81→    private var contentForState: some View {
    82→        switch viewModel.journeyState {
    83→        case .loading:
    84→            loadingView
    85→
    86→        case .noProfile:
    87→            setupProfileCard
    88→
    89→        case .workoutReady:
    90→            EmptyView()
    91→
    92→        case .workoutCompleted:
    93→            workoutCompletedCard
    94→
    95→        case .error(let message):
    96→            errorView(message: message)
    97→        }
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    // MARK: - Loading View
   101→
   102→    private var loadingView: some View {
   103→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   104→            ProgressView()
   105→                .tint(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
   106→            Text("common.loading".localized)
   107→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   108→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   109→        }
   110→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   111→        .frame(height: 200)
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    // MARK: - Setup Profile Card
   115→
   116→    private var setupProfileCard: some View {
   117→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   118→            Image(systemName: "person.crop.circle.badge.plus")
   119→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   120→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
   121→
   122→            Text("home.setup_profile.title".localized)
   123→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   124→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   125→
   126→            Text("home.setup_profile.subtitle".localized)
   127→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   128→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   129→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   130→
   131→            Button("home.setup_profile.cta".localized) {
   132→                router.push(.onboarding, on: .home)
   133→            }
   134→            .fitPrimaryStyle()
   135→        }
   136→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   137→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   138→        .background(FitTodayColor.surface)
   139→        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg))
   140→        .padding(.horizontal)
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    // MARK: - Workout Completed Card
   144→
   145→    private var workoutCompletedCard: some View {
   146→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   147→            Image(systemName: "checkmark.circle.fill")
   148→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   149→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.success)
   150→
   151→            Text("home.completed.title".localized)
   152→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   153→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   154→
   155→            Text("home.completed.subtitle".localized)
   156→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   157→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   158→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   159→        }
   160→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   161→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   162→        .background(FitTodayColor.success.opacity(0.1))
   163→        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg))
   164→        .overlay(
   165→            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.lg)
   166→                .stroke(FitTodayColor.success.opacity(0.3), lineWidth: 1)
   167→        )
   168→        .padding(.horizontal)
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    // MARK: - Error View
   172→
   173→    private func errorView(message: String) -> some View {
   174→        VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
   175→            Image(systemName: "exclamationmark.triangle.fill")
   176→                .font(.system(size: 48))
   177→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.error)
   178→
   179→            Text("home.error.title".localized)
   180→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 18, weight: .bold))
   181→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   182→
   183→            Text(message)
   184→                .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 14, weight: .medium))
   185→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   186→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
   187→
   188→            Button("common.retry".localized) {
   189→                Task { await viewModel.refresh() }
   190→            }
   191→            .fitSecondaryStyle()
   192→        }
   193→        .padding(FitTodaySpacing.lg)
   194→        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
   195→        .padding(.horizontal)
   196→    }
   197→}
   198→
   199→// MARK: - Preview
   200→
   201→#Preview {
   202→    let container = Container()
   203→    container.register(UserProfileRepository.self) { _ in MockUserProfileRepository() }
   204→    container.register(EntitlementRepository.self) { _ in MockEntitlementRepository() }
   205→    container.register(ProgramRepository.self) { _ in BundleProgramRepository() }
   206→    container.register(LibraryWorkoutsRepository.self) { _ in
   207→        BundleLibraryWorkoutsRepository()
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    return NavigationStack {
   211→        HomeView(resolver: container)
   212→            .environment(AppRouter())
   213→            .environment(WorkoutSessionStore(resolver: container))
   214→    }
   215→}
   216→
   217→// MARK: - Preview Mocks
   218→
   219→private final class MockUserProfileRepository: UserProfileRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
   220→    func loadProfile() async throws -> UserProfile? {
   221→        UserProfile(
   222→            mainGoal: .hypertrophy,
   223→            availableStructure: .fullGym,
   224→            preferredMethod: .traditional,
   225→            level: .intermediate,
   226→            healthConditions: [.none],
   227→            weeklyFrequency: 4
   228→        )
   229→    }
   230→
   231→    func saveProfile(_ profile: UserProfile) async throws {}
   232→}
   233→
   234→private final class MockEntitlementRepository: EntitlementRepository, @unchecked Sendable {
   235→    func currentEntitlement() async throws -> ProEntitlement { .free }
   236→    func entitlementStream() -> AsyncStream<ProEntitlement> {
   237→        AsyncStream { continuation in continuation.finish() }
   238→    }
   239→}

content.text:
# Claude Command: Commit

This command helps you create well-formatted commits with conventional commit messages and emoji.

## Usage

To create a commit, just type:
```
/commit
```

Or with options:
```
/commit --no-verify
```

## What This Command Does

1. Unless specified with `--no-verify`, automatically runs pre-commit checks:
   - `pnpm lint` to ensure code quality
   - `pnpm build` to verify the build succeeds
   - `pnpm generate:docs` to update documentation
2. Checks which files are staged with `git status`
3. If 0 files are staged, automatically adds all modified and new files with `git add`
4. Performs a `git diff` to understand what changes are being committed
5. Analyzes the diff to determine if multiple distinct logical changes are present
6. If multiple distinct changes are detected, suggests breaking the commit into multiple smaller commits
7. For each commit (or the single commit if not split), creates a commit message using emoji conventional commit format

## Best Practices for Commits

- **Verify before committing**: Ensure code is linted, builds correctly, and documentation is updated
- **Atomic commits**: Each commit should contain related changes that serve a single purpose
- **Split large changes**: If changes touch multiple concerns, split them into separate commits
- **Conventional commit format**: Use the format `<type>: <description>` where type is one of:
  - `feat`: A new feature
  - `fix`: A bug fix
  - `docs`: Documentation changes
  - `style`: Code style changes (formatting, etc)
  - `refactor`: Code changes that neither fix bugs nor add features
  - `perf`: Performance improvements
  - `test`: Adding or fixing tests
  - `chore`: Changes to the build process, tools, etc.
- **Present tense, imperative mood**: Write commit messages as commands (e.g., "add feature" not "added feature")
- **Concise first line**: Keep the first line under 72 characters
- **Emoji**: Each commit type is paired with an appropriate emoji:
  - ✨ `feat`: New feature
  - 🐛 `fix`: Bug fix
  - 📝 `docs`: Documentation
  - 💄 `style`: Formatting/style
  - ♻️ `refactor`: Code refactoring
  - ⚡️ `perf`: Performance improvements
  - ✅ `test`: Tests
  - 🔧 `chore`: Tooling, configuration
  - 🚀 `ci`: CI/CD improvements
  - 🗑️ `revert`: Reverting changes
  - 🧪 `test`: Add a failing test
  - 🚨 `fix`: Fix compiler/linter warnings
  - 🔒️ `fix`: Fix security issues
  - 👥 `chore`: Add or update contributors
  - 🚚 `refactor`: Move or rename resources
  - 🏗️ `refactor`: Make architectural changes
  - 🔀 `chore`: Merge branches
  - 📦️ `chore`: Add or update compiled files or packages
  - ➕ `chore`: Add a dependency
  - ➖ `chore`: Remove a dependency
  - 🌱 `chore`: Add or update seed files
  - 🧑‍💻 `chore`: Improve developer experience
  - 🧵 `feat`: Add or update code related to multithreading or concurrency
  - 🔍️ `feat`: Improve SEO
  - 🏷️ `feat`: Add or update types
  - 💬 `feat`: Add or update text and literals
  - 🌐 `feat`: Internationalization and localization
  - 👔 `feat`: Add or update business logic
  - 📱 `feat`: Work on responsive design
  - 🚸 `feat`: Improve user experience / usability
  - 🩹 `fix`: Simple fix for a non-critical issue
  - 🥅 `fix`: Catch errors
  - 👽️ `fix`: Update code due to external API changes
  - 🔥 `fix`: Remove code or files
  - 🎨 `style`: Improve structure/format of the code
  - 🚑️ `fix`: Critical hotfix
  - 🎉 `chore`: Begin a project
  - 🔖 `chore`: Release/Version tags
  - 🚧 `wip`: Work in progress
  - 💚 `fix`: Fix CI build
  - 📌 `chore`: Pin dependencies to specific versions
  - 👷 `ci`: Add or update CI build system
  - 📈 `feat`: Add or update analytics or tracking code
  - ✏️ `fix`: Fix typos
  - ⏪️ `revert`: Revert changes
  - 📄 `chore`: Add or update license
  - 💥 `feat`: Introduce breaking changes
  - 🍱 `assets`: Add or update assets
  - ♿️ `feat`: Improve accessibility
  - 💡 `docs`: Add or update comments in source code
  - 🗃️ `db`: Perform database related changes
  - 🔊 `feat`: Add or update logs
  - 🔇 `fix`: Remove logs
  - 🤡 `test`: Mock things
  - 🥚 `feat`: Add or update an easter egg
  - 🙈 `chore`: Add or update .gitignore file
  - 📸 `test`: Add or update snapshots
  - ⚗️ `experiment`: Perform experiments
  - 🚩 `feat`: Add, update, or remove feature flags
  - 💫 `ui`: Add or update animations and transitions
  - ⚰️ `refactor`: Remove dead code
  - 🦺 `feat`: Add or update code related to validation
  - ✈️ `feat`: Improve offline support

## Guidelines for Splitting Commits

When analyzing the diff, consider splitting commits based on these criteria:

1. **Different concerns**: Changes to unrelated parts of the codebase
2. **Different types of changes**: Mixing features, fixes, refactoring, etc.
3. **File patterns**: Changes to different types of files (e.g., source code vs documentation)
4. **Logical grouping**: Changes that would be easier to understand or review separately
5. **Size**: Very large changes that would be clearer if broken down

## Examples

Good commit messages:
- ✨ feat: add user authentication system
- 🐛 fix: resolve memory leak in rendering process
- 📝 docs: update API documentation with new endpoints
- ♻️ refactor: simplify error handling logic in parser
- 🚨 fix: resolve linter warnings in component files
- 🧑‍💻 chore: improve developer tooling setup process
- 👔 feat: implement business logic for transaction validation
- 🩹 fix: address minor styling inconsistency in header
- 🚑️ fix: patch critical security vulnerability in auth flow
- 🎨 style: reorganize component structure for better readability
- 🔥 fix: remove deprecated legacy code
- 🦺 feat: add input validation for user registration form
- 💚 fix: resolve failing CI pipeline tests
- 📈 feat: implement analytics tracking for user engagement
- 🔒️ fix: strengthen authentication password requirements
- ♿️ feat: improve form accessibility for screen readers

Example of splitting commits:
- First commit: ✨ feat: add new solc version type definitions
- Second commit: 📝 docs: update documentation for new solc versions
- Third commit: 🔧 chore: update package.json dependencies
- Fourth commit: 🏷️ feat: add type definitions for new API endpoints
- Fifth commit: 🧵 feat: improve concurrency handling in worker threads
- Sixth commit: 🚨 fix: resolve linting issues in new code
- Seventh commit: ✅ test: add unit tests for new solc version features
- Eighth commit: 🔒️ fix: update dependencies with security vulnerabilities

## Command Options

- `--no-verify`: Skip running the pre-commit checks (lint, build, generate:docs)

## Important Notes

- By default, pre-commit checks (`pnpm lint`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm generate:docs`) will run to ensure code quality
- If these checks fail, you'll be asked if you want to proceed with the commit anyway or fix the issues first
- If specific files are already staged, the command will only commit those files
- If no files are staged, it will automatically stage all modified and new files
- The commit message will be constructed based on the changes detected
- Before committing, the command will review the diff to identify if multiple commits would be more appropriate
- If suggesting multiple commits, it will help you stage and commit the changes separately
- Always reviews the commit diff to ensure the message matches the changes
- Do NOT add Claude co-authorship footer to commits
I'll analyze the changes and create the commit.

struct TrainerDashboardView: View {
    let trainer: PersonalTrainer
    let workouts: [TrainerWorkout]
    let isChatEnabled: Bool
    let onDisconnect: () -> Void
    let initialTab: TrainerDashboardTab

    @State private var selectedTab: TrainerDashboardTab

    init(
        trainer: PersonalTrainer,
        workouts: [TrainerWorkout],
        isChatEnabled: Bool,
        onDisconnect: @escaping () -> Void,
        initialTab: TrainerDashboardTab = .today
    ) {
        self.trainer = trainer
        self.workouts = workouts
        self.isChatEnabled = isChatEnabled
        self.onDisconnect = onDisconnect
        self.initialTab = initialTab
        self._selectedTab = State(wrappedValue: initialTab)
    }

input.command:
gh pr create --repo Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday --base main --head feature/reorganize-screens-and-flows --title "feat: Personal trainer Home flow — card, routes, today tab" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **HomeViewModel** now loads personal trainer state (current trainer + today's workout) in parallel with programs, with real-time observation of relationship changes
- **AppRouter** gains `.trainerDashboard` and `.trainerChat` routes with deep link support (`fittoday://trainer/dashboard`, `fittoday://trainer/chat`)
- **TrainerDashboardView** gets a new "Hoje" (Today) tab as the default tab, showing today's trainer-assigned workout with exercise preview and empty state
- **PersonalTrainerHomeCard** component added to HomeView with two states: discover mode (CTA to find trainers) and connected mode (trainer info, today's workout badge, chat/workout quick actions)
- All features are gated behind `personalTrainerEnabled` feature flag — invisible when flag is off

## Issues Addressed

- Closes #28 — Card de personal trainer na Home
- Closes #29 — HomeViewModel: carregar estado do personal trainer
- Closes #30 — Rotas de acesso direto ao Dashboard e Chat
- Closes #31 — TrainerDashboard aba "Hoje" com treino do dia
- Related: #32 (meta-issue for the complete flow)
- Related: #27 (feature flags — already enabled in Firebase)

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify card does NOT appear when `personalTrainerEnabled` flag is `false`
- [ ] Verify "discover" card appears when flag is `true` and no trainer connected
- [ ] Tap "Ver personais disponíveis" navigates to `PersonalTrainerView`
- [ ] Verify "connected" card shows trainer photo, name, and status when connected
- [ ] Verify "Treino de hoje disponível" badge appears when CMS workout is scheduled for today
- [ ] Tap "Ver treino" navigates to `CMSWorkoutDetailView`
- [ ] Tap "Chat" navigates to dashboard on chat tab
- [ ] Tap trainer card navigates to `TrainerDashboardView` on "Hoje" tab
- [ ] Verify deep link `fittoday://trainer/dashboard` opens dashboard
- [ ] Verify deep link `fittoday://trainer/chat` opens dashboard on chat tab
- [ ] Verify "Hoje" tab shows empty state when no workout scheduled for today
- [ ] Verify "Hoje" tab shows workout details when workout is scheduled
- [ ] Verify no regressions on existing routes (`.personalTrainer`, `.trainerSearch`)
EOF
)"

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's a summary:

## Summary

**PR created:** https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/pull/34

### What was implemented (4 issues):

| Issue | What | Key Changes |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **#29** | HomeViewModel PT state | Added `PersonalTrainerHomeCardState`, loads trainer + today's workout in parallel, real-time observation |
| **#30** | Navigation routes | `.trainerDashboard` + `.trainerChat` routes, deep links `fittoday://trainer/dashboard` and `/chat`, `initialTab` parameter |
| **#31** | "Hoje" tab | `TodayWorkoutTabView` with workout preview + empty state, `TrainerDashboardTab` enum with Today as default |
| **#28** | Home card | `PersonalTrainerHomeCard` with discover/connected states, integrated in `HomeView` after `DailyStatsCard` |

### Files modified/created:
- `HomeViewModel.swift` — PT state, feature flag, parallel loading, observation
- `AppRouter.swift` — 2 new routes + deep link parsing
- `TabRootView.swift` — Route registration with `initialTab`
- `TrainerDashboardView.swift` — Tab enum refactored, Today tab added, `initialTab` support
- `PersonalTrainerView.swift` — `initialTab` passthrough
- **New:** `TodayWorkoutTabView.swift` — Today's workout tab content
- **New:** `PersonalTrainerHomeCard.swift` — Home card component
- `HomeView.swift` — Card integration

Build verified successfully (0 errors). The PR will close issues #28-#31 when merged. After merge, those issues can be closed via GitHub's auto-close mechanism (the `Closes #XX` references in the commit messages and PR body).

Foi encontrado um erro na execução da verificação dos personais, https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/issues/33, a investigação sugere,
Revisar as Firestore Security Rules para as coleções personalWorkouts e trainerStudents
Verificar se o token de autenticação está sendo injetado corretamente no header das requisições ao CMS
Confirmar se o studentId está mapeado corretamente para o userId esperado pelas regras do Firestore
Checar se houve alguma mudança recente nas rules ou no middleware de autenticação do CMS
Realize esse check e faça o commit no PR que foi aberto agora.
